what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a paragraph comment by Tim) that responds to another board item and argues for typed, registry-backed intent while sketching a long-press mobile UI gesture for selecting an annotation type.
observations: YAML frontmatter follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type: note, author_session: tim, channel: dragnet-development, and a commented_on link to board://item-8c087208
Body is an informal, spoken-style paragraph rather than a structured spec or code
Proposes that intent be typed, live in a registry, and be linked to an annotation action
Explores a mobile interaction: long-press the save/send button to open a menu of possible annotation types
Created and updated timestamps are identical: 2026-06-24T09:29:20.417374+00:00
standouts: The central claim: intent should change the envelope's downstream contract, not just act as a tag
A concrete mobile UX proposal: click-and-hold save/send to reveal and select the intended annotation type
It explicitly links to a prior item via commented_on
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with board:// URI addressing and ISO 8601 timestamps
Uses the links field to record a commented_on relationship
Body uses informal, first-person design voice rather than a formal specification
concerns: The UX proposal is speculative ('I don't know', 'maybe') and leaves open questions
Terms like 'annotation action' and the registry schema are not defined in this file
No mention of validation, accessibility, or conflict with a normal tap on the save/send button
notes: Tim's paragraph note pushing for typed, registry-backed intent and a long-press mobile gesture to choose annotation type.
questions: What is the exact schema of the proposed intent registry?
What are the allowed annotation types or actions?
How does a long-press save/send button interact with accessibility and single-tap expectations?
What is the precise relationship between 'intent' and 'annotation action'?
What does the parent item item-8c087208 say?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and type label it as a paragraph note; the body is in fact a design reply/comment, which aligns with the commented_on link but is not explicitly marked as a reply.
intention_signals: It is trying to advance a design where user intent is formally typed and stored, and where selecting an intent/annotation type on mobile is made low-friction through a long-press gesture on the send/save button.
novelty: It merges a semantic-design argument (intent changes contract) with a specific mobile interaction mechanic (long-press save/send to choose type) in one informal note.
contribution: Records an early concrete UX idea (long-press send/save to choose annotation type) and a semantic principle (intent as a contract-changing type in a registry) for downstream design fusion.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development commenting on item-8c087208, proposing that intent be typed in a registry and linked to annotation actions, and sketching a long-press mobile save/send button interaction to select the type.
intention_for_embedding: To advance the design of typed, registry-backed intent and a low-friction mobile UI for choosing annotation types.
